summary.intent: Enforce a maximum Rust file size of 1,500 lines across the moltis codebase with a CI check to prevent new violations.
summary.outcome: Created scripts/check-file-size.sh with an allowlist of 31 existing oversized files, wired it into CI (fmt job) and local-validate.sh, and documented the rule in CLAUDE.md.

repo: The moltis workspace contains ~304K total lines of Rust across its crates.
31 files exceed 1,500 lines; 67 files exceed 1,000 lines — widespread large-file pattern.
chat/src/lib.rs is by far the largest at 15,755 lines, nearly double the next largest file.
CI check scripts follow the pattern in scripts/check-changelog-guard.sh — bash scripts invoked from the fmt job in .github/workflows/ci.yml.
There is a scripts/local-validate.sh that mirrors CI checks for local use.
Worktrees exist in the repo and must be excluded when searching for Rust files.

code.finding: 15,755 lines — largest file, highest priority for decomposition.
9,340 lines — second largest, likely a domain decomposition candidate.
New CI script enforcing 1,500-line max with an allowlist for existing violations.
File-size check added to the fmt job alongside other check scripts.
1,500-line file size rule documented here for contributor awareness.

workflow: Neither clippy nor rustfmt provide a file-size lint; a custom bash CI script is the standard enforcement mechanism.
Well-maintained Rust projects (tokio, serde, axum) target ~1,000 lines/file; 1,500 is a pragmatic ceiling for large codebases.
An allowlist approach lets you enforce the rule immediately without blocking existing work, removing entries incrementally as files are split.

friction: A security hook flagged the CI file edit as potentially unsafe, requiring a re-edit to proceed.
Initial script missed one allowlist file, requiring a fix and re-test before committing.
open_items: 31 files in the allowlist still exceed 1,500 lines and need to be split by domain — no schedule set.
chat/src/lib.rs at 15,755 lines is the highest-priority decomposition target.
agents/src/runner.rs (9,340 lines) and gateway/src/server.rs (6,054 lines) are next in priority.
The 1,500-line threshold was chosen pragmatically — revisit whether to tighten to 1,000 lines as the allowlist shrinks.
